aliases: Chicken Soup with Bacon and Ranch

english: Chicken Soup with Bacon and Ranch

food_subgroup: Soup-based meal with poultry and processed meat

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general chicken soup class (FOODON_00002372). The specific Chicken Bacon Ranch Soup Meal with bacon and ranch dressing components is not individually represented; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers soups and broths as a category. No specific code exists for chicken bacon ranch soup; closest_parent category used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this specific commercial soup meal product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard meal serving of Chicken Bacon Ranch Soup is approximately 450 g, comprising a bowl of creamy chicken soup with bacon pieces and ranch-seasoned broth, as typically served at Subway Singapore outlets.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken Noodle Soup, canned, condensed — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; adapted with bacon and ranch dressing components using USDA Foundation Foods entries for cooked chicken breast, cooked bacon, and ranch dressing
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for Chicken Bacon Ranch Soup as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated by component-weighted averaging: approximately 55% chicken broth base (USDA FDC #171534 Chicken broth), 25% cooked chicken breast (USDA FDC #331960), 10% cooked bacon (USDA FDC #168318), and 10% ranch dressing (USDA FDC #172539). Values represent a creamy, moderately caloric soup meal per 100 g of the combined dish. Sodium is elevated due to broth and bacon contributions. All component sources are USDA FoodData Central approved sources.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — chicken soup and cream-based soups; analogous estimate using chicken vegetable soup GI reference
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Chicken Bacon Ranch Soup. GI estimated at 38 based on analogous cream-based chicken soup entries in the University of Sydney GI Database (chicken vegetable soup GI ~36–40, low GI category). The low carbohydrate content (~5.4 g/100 g) and protein-fat matrix suppress glycemic response. Glycemic load per 450 g serving = (38 × 24.3 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 9.2; rounded to 11.7 accounting for full meal accompaniments. GL calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (38 × 30.8) / 100 ≈ 11.7 for the full 450 g meal serving.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from bacon, broth, and ranch dressing
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish due to its high sodium content of approximately 480 mg per 100 g (approximately 2160 mg per 450 g serving), which approaches or exceeds the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg in a single meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate load with low glycemic index; saturated fat from bacon and cream
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low GI (~38) and moderate carbohydrate content make this soup relatively manageable for blood glucose control, but individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor portion size and be aware of the saturated fat contribution from bacon and cream, which may affect insulin sensitivity over time.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium, saturated fat, and cholesterol from bacon, cream, and ranch dressing
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This meal is high in sodium (~2160 mg per serving) and contains saturated fat (~9.5 g per serving) and cholesterol (~126 mg per serving) from bacon and cream components, making it a high-concern food for individuals managing cardiovascular disease who should limit intake and consider lower-sodium alternatives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Bacon Ranch Soup Meal is a Western-style creamy soup dish served as a complete meal, available at Subway outlets in Singapore and similar fast-casual sandwich restaurants. The soup is composed of a rich, cream-based chicken broth flavoured with ranch seasoning, containing tender pieces of cooked chicken breast, crispy bacon bits, and aromatic herbs and spices. The texture is smooth and hearty with savoury, smoky, and tangy flavour notes from the ranch dressing and bacon. As a meal offering, it is typically served with bread or a side, making it a filling option for lunch or dinner. Nutritionally, the dish provides a moderate protein content (~7.2 g/100 g) from chicken and bacon, with a notable sodium load (~480 mg/100 g) due to the broth, bacon, and ranch dressing components. The fat content (~5.8 g/100 g) includes saturated fat from cream and bacon. The glycemic index is estimated at 38 (low GI), making it relatively suitable for blood glucose management in moderate portions, though the high sodium content is a significant concern for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The dish is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those following halal dietary requirements without specific halal-certified preparation. It contains common allergens including milk, wheat, soy, and eggs. In the Singapore food delivery context, this meal is priced at approximately SGD 15.20 and is served by Subway and similar Western fast-casual outlets.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry exists for Chicken Bacon Ranch Soup Meal in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or any other approved primary source. Nutrient values are estimated by component-weighted averaging of USDA FoodData Central approved entries for chicken broth, cooked chicken breast, cooked bacon, and ranch dressing. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using comparable cream-based chicken soup entries. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach rather than a direct food match.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reliably reported across the component USDA FoodData Central entries used for this composite estimation and cannot be confidently assigned. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not consistently reported in the component sources used for this composite dish estimation. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary component sources used for this estimation. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and trans fat are estimated from component sources and carry moderate uncertainty. The serving size of 450 g is an estimate based on typical fast-casual soup meal portions; actual restaurant serving size may vary. Halal status is listed as false as Subway Singapore's halal certification status for this specific product requires verification at point of purchase. The food_id GD000369 is preserved from the input seed record. This record was generated from field survey data data and processed under Project Veritas v2. Restaurants identified: Subway - Funan and Funan - Mix & Match (2 outlets). Price: SGD 15.20.
